Public Works Commission Agenda Report

To: Chair Simmens  and  Members  of the Public Works Commission Prepared  by: Troy Spayd, Assistant Public  Works Director/City Engineer Approved  by: Rob  DuBoux,  Public Works Director/City Engineer Date prepared: August 20, 2021 Meeting  date: August 25, 2021 Subject: Capital  Improvement  Projects and  Disaster Recovery  Projects Status Report
RECOMMENDED ACTION: Receive  and  file report  on  the status of the  City’s current and upcoming  Capital  Improvements  Projects and  Disaster Recovery Projects.

DISCUSSION:   Staff  will  provide  a  status  update  on  the  following  active  projects  in  the Fiscal Year  2021-2022  Capital  Improvement  Program:

Capital Improvement Projects (Design)

Marie Canyon Green Streets

¾ Updates:

  The  City  has  recently  received  Measure  W  funds  to  complete  this project.

Staff  is  working  on  the  project  plans  to  prepare  for  public bidding. ¾ Project Schedule:  Complete  Design: September  2021

 Begin Construction:  Winter 2021

¾

Civic Center Water Treatment Facility Phase 2

¾ Updates:

 Project engineer  is preparing  the 90%  design documents.

 The  City  obtained  a  project  CDP  from  Planning  Commission  on February  1,

 2021.

 The  State  has approved  the  City’s application  for the  State  Revolving Fund  program.

¾

Capital Improvement Projects (Construction)

Civic Center Way Improvements

Civic Center  Way Improvements ¾ Updates:

  The  contractor  continues  to  maintain  the  new  planting  material during the establishment  period per the contract documents. This period ends at the end  of August 2021.

Staff will be  closing out the project.

Malibu Park Drainage Improvements

¾ Project  Description:

  This  project  consists  of  storm  drain  repairs  within  the Malibu  Park  neighborhood.  These  repairs  are  the  result of  damage  caused by the  subsequent  storms after the  Woolsey  Fire.

Work will include  clearing and  grubbing,  demolition,  cold  milling, removal  of  existing corrugated  metal pipe, new asphalt berms/dikes, pavement  repairs, pavement  overlay, striping, new  reinforced  concrete pipes, and  rock slope  protection.

¾ Updates:

  Construction  started  at  the  Morning  View  location  and  included installing  a  new  60"  HDPE  pipe  and  constructing  a  new  inlet  basin  and headwall.  The contractor will also begin working at the other locations per the project  plans  and  specifications.  Work  will  include  temporary  lane  closures with flaggers.

¾ Project Schedule:  August 2021  through  October 2021

2021 Annual Street Maintenance

2021  Annual  Street Maintenance ¾ Project  Description:

    This  project  provides  for  the  reconstruction  and maintenance  of  City  streets.  Including  asphalt  pavement  repairs,  Type  II slurry seal, speed  humps  and new  thermoplastic  striping.

¾ Updates:

  City  Council  awarded  the  contract  at  the  July  12th  meeting  to Pavement  Coatings Co.  City staff is currently working with  the contractor  on project related  submittals.

¾ Project  Schedule:

  Begin  construction  the  last week  of  August  2021  through October  2021

Vehicle Protection Devices

¾ Project  Description:

   Per  Malibu  Municipal  Code  Section  17.48.070  requires vehicle impact protection devices to be installed for all parking spaces located adjacent  to  any  outdoor  pedestrian  seating  area.    There  are  two  locations within  the  City’s right-of-way  and  City-owned  property  that  would  require  a vehicle impact protection  device.

¾ Estimated  Schedule:

 Complete  Design: Summer  2021

 Begin Construction:  Fall 2021

Outdoor Warning Sirens (FEMA Project)

¾ Updates:

    This project  will be  funded  through  a  grant  from  FEMA  after  the Wooley  Fire.  Staff  received  proposals  from  consultants  to  design  and  to develop  a  plan  that  includes  the  evaluation  of  a  siren  system  and  possible locations.

It is anticipated  a consultant  will be hired in September  2021.

¾ Project Schedule:

 Complete  Design: Unknown

Storm Drain Master Plan (FEMA Project)

¾ Project  Description:

   This project  will be  funded  through  a grant from  FEMA after  the  Woolsey  Fire.

The  City  will  create  a  complete  inventory  of  storm drains,  culverts,  debris  basins,  manholes,  and  other  drainage  structures within  the  City.    Staff  released  a  request  for  qualifications/proposals  from qualified firms.

   Proposals  are due  in September  2021.

¾ Updates:

    An  RFQ  was  send  out  on  August  19,  2021  seeking  qualified consultants  to develop  the master plan.

¾ Estimated  Schedule:

 Completion  Date:  Spring 2022

Disaster Projects (Completed)

Guardrail Replacement Citywide (FEMA Project)

¾ Project  Description:

    This  project  consisted  of  replacing  the  damaged guardrail throughout  the City as a result of the Woolsey Fire.

Corral Canyon Road Bridge Repairs (FEMA Project)

¾ Project Description:

  This project consisted of replacing fire damaged  existing fencing and repairing  the damaged  embankment  adjacent  to the bridge.

Corral Canyon Culvert Repairs (FEMA Project)

¾ Project Description:  This project has been cancelled as it could not get FEMA approval.

Disaster Projects (Not Started)

Clover Heights Storm Drain (FEMA Project)

Page 5 of 6
Agenda Item # 4.A.

¾ Project Description:

   This project consists of design and  construction of a few storm drains on Clover  Heights.  The existing storm drain  facility ends  at the intersection  of  Clover  Heights  and  Harvester  Road.
During  storms,  this intersection  floods  and  causes  debris to block the  road.   An  extended  storm drain towards  the end  of Clover Heights will help  eliminate  this issue.

¾

Estimated  Schedule:

 Complete  Design: Spring 2022  Begin Construction:

  Summer  2022

Latigo Canyon Road Culvert Repairs (FEMA Project)

¾ Project Description:

  This project consists of repairing the existing storm drain on Latigo Canyon  Road  located  approximately  2,500  feet from PCH that was damaged  by the Woolsey  Fire.

¾

Estimated  Schedule:  Complete  Design: Winter 2021

 Begin Construction:  Spring 2022
